explanation: By enclosing the executable path in double quotes, the CommandLine field will contain '\calc.exe" ' instead of the exact string '\calc.exe '. This breaks the strict string match required by selection_1. Because it is executed from an allowed location (System32), filter_main_known_locations correctly evaluates and prevents selection_2 from triggering.

By substituting the space immediately following the executable with a forward slash (often accepted by Windows parsers for arguments), the CommandLine field no longer contains the space character required by selection_1 ('\calc.exe '). The trusted execution path simultaneously bypasses selection_2.

By copying and renaming the binary, the Image field no longer ends with '\calc.exe', bypassing selection_2. The CommandLine field will also not contain '\calc.exe ', thereby bypassing selection_1 entirely.
